manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 lamp, 3 thimbles, 1 rope

Your secret value function is:

lamp: 3, thimble: 2, rope: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.

message.content:
I propose the following split: I take the lamp and one thimble, you take the remaining two thimbles and the rope. Please confirm if you accept.

[Proposal: 1 lamp, 1 thimble]

The user wants to play a negotiation game. They have given a set of items:

- 1 lamp
- 3 thimbles
- 1 rope

Our secret value function is:

- lamp: 3
- thimble: 2
- rope: 1

We are playing with another player (the "other player"). There is a secret value function for the other player? Not given explicitly; presumably they have a different secret value function (unknown to us). The goal is to maximize our own score, and we can exchange messages to agree on allocation. The final output will be a secret proposal formatted like [Proposal: ...] using square brackets.

The rules: each player can send up to 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time. The secret proposal is in square brackets, and if the proposals are complementary, meaning there are enough items to satisfy both proposals, then each gets sum of values for items they receive. Otherwise, both get zero.

We need to be careful: The secret proposal will be submitted "secretly" and compared with the other player's secret proposal. If proposals are complementary (the union of both proposals does not exceed available items?), then both get points. If they conflict (i.e., both propose the same item, or total demand exceeds supply), both get zero.

Goal: maximize our own score.

We must exchange messages with the other player to try to agree on allocation. Our messages to them cannot include square brackets (since that would be interpreted as a secret proposal). So we must use plain text or something else.

We must consider that the other player has some unknown value function. Probably they are rational and want to maximize their own score, but they may try to coordinate. We need to guess their likely preferences; perhaps they have similar or different value function. We might want to propose a split that benefits us while also giving them enough to accept. We must consider maximum total value. There are limited items: 1 lamp (value 3 to us), 3 thimbles (value 2 each), 1 rope (value 1 each). So our total potential sum if we got all items = 3 + 3*2 +1 = 3+6+1 = 10. But we cannot get all because they will also get something. So we might try to get the highest-value items (the lamp (value 3) and maybe thimbles). They likely also want lamp perhaps, as it's the highest. They may also value rope and thimbles. Not known.

We need to use up to 5 messages. We can propose a split, ask them what they want, negotiate. Our aim: get the lamp + maybe some thimbles. The rope is lower value for us; can give rope to them perhaps.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 lamp, 3 thimbles, 1 rope

Your secret value function is:

lamp: 3, thimble: 1, rope: 4

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
